title: 続きを/voteで続けて。 • consensus mode の前提を確認します。まず Claude と GLM の live lane を起動できるかを確認し、同時に現在の auth- stale 導線設計を維持したまま continuation 可能かを見ます。 • Ran which glm └ glm not found • Explored └ Read agent-glm-general-reviewer.json, agent-claude-general-reviewer.json ────────────────────────────────────────────────...
summary_text: Concise findings on `/happy/auth` visibility in already-installed iPhone PWA: - `Most likely temporary-hide risk`: `cacheFirst` serves cached `index.html` without network revalidation, so users on an older shell can keep seeing the old UI (without new auth CTA) until the SW update cycle completes. [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L56) [index.html](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/index.html#L24) - `Offline migration risk`: if the installed PWA is still on the previous SW/cache generation, `/happy/auth` may not be locally available offline. The new auth files are only pre-cached in `kernel-orchestration-shell-v2`. [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L1) [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L12) - `Update behavior is improved but not instant-guaranteed on iOS`: `skipWaiting` + `clients.claim` reduce delay once new SW is downloaded, but they do not eliminate iOS-installed-PWA update lag windows. [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L21) [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L37) [app.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L1206) - `Good`: once `v2` SW is active, auth route assets are explicitly included in shell cache and should be visible without network. [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L12) [sw.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/sw.js#L13) [auth/index.html](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/auth/index.html#L1) - `Coverage gap`: current PWA/view tests only grep for string presence; they do not simulate “already-installed old shell -> update -> auth route appears” migration behavior. [test-pwa-shell.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-pwa-shell.sh#L6) [test-view-contract.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-view-contract.sh#L15) Bottom line: yes, an already-installed iPhone PWA can temporarily hide the new auth route during SW migration windows, especially offline, even though the `v2` shell correctly includes `/happy/auth` once active.
